jsx.contract: Shared JsxPlan intent emits TSX/typed createElement or classic React-compatible createElement; disabled required runtimes fail before output.

exactSourceMapsAndBudgets.contract: Two clean TS/classic compiler trees hash identically after documented path normalization; exact tokens and executable stacks resolve through their owned source-map stages; module/temp/import growth and output-size ceilings require reviewed manifest changes.

loweringPlans.contract: TempPlan owns iterator and expression-result bindings; NamePlan owns TVar-based collision/readability names. TS and classic printers consume immutable lookups while semantic traces, clean tree hashes, collision fixtures, and no-temp budgets guard the projections.

importAttributes.contract: Dependency planning retains the import attribute independently of printer syntax; TS and classic ESM both emit the JSON loader contract and execute the same checked-in module on supported Node releases.

importAttributeValidation.contract: A handwritten import attribute must appear once with one non-empty string literal. Both implementation profiles reject malformed metadata during dependency planning and preserve the prior public output tree.

stringLiterals.contract: Standard Haxe is the primary runtime oracle; classic Genes and genes-ts must preserve exact UTF-16 code units, parseable source, metadata spelling, and Haxe source-map provenance. A live original Genes checkout is a secondary oracle when available.
